title_en: As-Salahi: Life and the Ambition of a Comprehensive Seerah
title_id: As-Salahi: Kehidupan dan Ambisi Sirah yang Komprehensif
content_id:
Muhammad ibn Yusuf as-Salahi ad-Dimashqi (wafat 942 H / 1535 M) hidup dan bekerja di Damaskus Ottoman pada masa ketika kota itu tetap menjadi pusat utama keilmuan Islam meskipun kekuasaan politik telah berpindah ke Konstantinopel. Pembentukan ilmiahnya sangat tradisional — pelatihan ekstensif dalam hadis, tafsir al-Quran, fikih, dan ilmu biografi — dan ia mewarisi budaya keilmuan kaya Damaskus yang telah melahirkan Ibn Katsir dua abad sebelumnya. As-Salahi adalah seorang penyusun dan ulama yang produktif, namun Subul al-Huda war-Rashad fi Sirat Khayr al-Ibad (Jalan-jalan Bimbingan yang Benar dalam Biografi Makhluk Terbaik) tidak diragukan lagi merupakan magnum opus-nya dan karya yang menjadi fondasi reputasinya yang abadi.

Ambisi di balik Subul al-Huda war-Rashad tidak lain adalah pengkajian biografi kenabian paling komprehensif yang pernah dicoba. Mencakup dua belas jilid besar dalam bentuk lengkapnya, karya ini bertujuan untuk mengintegrasikan setiap narasi yang telah diautentikasi tentang kehidupan, karakteristik, mukjizat, dan peristiwa misi Nabi, yang diambil dari seluruh rentang literatur sirah, hadis, dan sejarah yang tersedia bagi seorang ulama abad ke-10 H. As-Salahi sangat sadar bekerja dalam tradisi yang telah mapan — ia mengenal dan mengandalkan Ibn Hisham, Ibn Sa'd, Ibn Katsir, dan puluhan pengarang sirah terdahulu lainnya — namun tujuannya adalah sintesis dan kelengkapan daripada reduksi kritis.

Judul karya ini sendiri memiliki makna penting. Subul al-Huda war-Rashad (Jalan-jalan Bimbingan yang Benar) membingkai biografi kenabian bukan sekadar sebagai informasi historis tetapi sebagai panduan bagi kehidupan Muslim. Pendekatan as-Salahi mencerminkan pandangan, yang tersebar luas dalam keilmuan Islam, bahwa mengetahui kehidupan Nabi secara rinci bukan merupakan latihan akademis melainkan kewajiban agama dengan konsekuensi praktis langsung: semakin lengkap seseorang mengenal teladan kenabian, semakin efektif ia dapat mengikutinya. Orientasi poblas ini membentuk karya sepanjang isinya, memberinya kualitas komprehensivitas yang bersifat devosional sekaligus keluasan historis.

As-Salahi wafat di Damaskus pada tahun 942 H, meninggalkan karya yang telah selesai. Sejarah transmisi manuskrip, pencetakan, dan penerimaan akademisnya selanjutnya mencerminkan dinamika kompleks pembelajaran Islam era Utsmani dan munculnya budaya cetak secara bertahap di dunia berbahasa Arab.
content_en:
Muhammad ibn Yusuf as-Salahi ad-Dimashqi (d. 942 AH / 1535 CE) lived and worked in Ottoman Damascus during a period when the city remained a major center of Islamic scholarship despite the shift of political power to Constantinople. His scholarly formation was thoroughly traditional — extensive training in hadith, Quran exegesis, jurisprudence, and the biographical sciences — and he inherited the rich scholarly culture of Damascus that had produced Ibn Kathir two centuries earlier. As-Salahi was a prolific compiler and scholar, but Subul al-Huda war-Rashad fi Sirat Khayr al-Ibad (The Paths of Right Guidance in the Biography of the Best of Creation) is undoubtedly his magnum opus and the work on which his lasting reputation rests.

The ambition behind Subul al-Huda war-Rashad was nothing less than the most comprehensive treatment of prophetic biography ever attempted. Spanning twelve substantial volumes in its complete form, the work aimed to incorporate every authenticated narrative about the Prophet's life, characteristics, miracles, and the events of his mission, drawn from the entire range of seerah, hadith, and historical literature available to a 10th-century AH scholar. As-Salahi was deeply conscious of working within an established tradition — he knew and drew on Ibn Hisham, Ibn Sa'd, Ibn Kathir, and dozens of other earlier seerah authors — but his goal was synthesis and comprehensiveness rather than critical reduction.

The title of the work is itself significant. Subul al-Huda war-Rashad (Paths of Right Guidance) frames the prophetic biography not merely as historical information but as a guide for Muslim life. As-Salahi's approach reflects the view, widespread in Islamic scholarship, that knowing the Prophet's life in detail is not an academic exercise but a religious duty with direct practical consequences: the more fully one knows the prophetic example, the more effectively one can follow it. This pietistic orientation shapes the work throughout, giving it a quality of devotional comprehensiveness as well as historical breadth.

As-Salahi died in Damascus in 942 AH, leaving the work complete. Its subsequent history of manuscript transmission, printing, and scholarly reception reflects the complex dynamics of Ottoman-era Islamic learning and the gradual emergence of print culture in the Arabic-speaking world.
content_ar: محمد ﷺ ibn يوسف ﷺ as-Salahi ad-Dimashqi (d. 942 AH / 1535 CE) lived and worked in Ottoman Damascus خلال a period عندما the city remained a major center of Islamic scholarship رغم the shift of political power to Constantinople. His العلمي formation was thoroughly التقليدي — extensive training in الحديث, القرآن الكريم exegesis, jurisprudence, and the biographical sciences — and he inherited the rich العلمي culture of Damascus that had produced Ibn Kathir two centuries earlier. As-Salahi was a prolific compiler and scholar, but Subul al-Huda war-Rashad fi الصراط Khayr al-Ibad (The Paths of الحق الهداية in the Biography of the Best of Creation) is undoubtedly his magnum opus and the work on which his lasting reputation rests. The ambition behind Subul al-Huda war-Rashad was nothing less than the most comprehensive treatment of prophetic biography ever attempted. Spanning twelve substantial volumes in its complete form, the work aimed to incorporate every authenticated narrative about النبي ﷺ's الحياة, characteristics, miracles, and the events of his mission, drawn from the entire range of seerah, الحديث, and historical literature available to a 10th-century AH scholar. As-Salahi was deeply conscious of working within an established التراث — he knew and drew on Ibn Hisham, Ibn Sa'd, Ibn Kathir, and dozens of other earlier seerah authors — but his goal was synthesis and comprehensiveness rather than البالغ الأهمية reduction. The title of the work is itself المهم. Subul al-Huda war-Rashad (Paths of الحق الهداية) frames the prophetic biography not merely as historical information but as a guide for مسلم الحياة. As-Salahi's approach reflects the view, widespread in Islamic scholarship, that knowing النبي ﷺ's الحياة in detail is not an الأكاديمي exercise but a religious الواجب with direct practical consequences: the more fully one knows the prophetic example, the more effectively one can follow it. This pietistic orientation shapes the work throughout, giving it a quality of devotional comprehensiveness as well as historical breadth. As-Salahi died in Damascus in 942 AH, leaving the work complete. Its subsequent history of manuscript transmission, printing, and العلمي reception reflects the complex dynamics of Ottoman-era Islamic learning and the gradual emergence of print culture in the Arabic-speaking الدنيا.
